explanation: The user instruction 'turn around' means the robot should rotate 180 degrees. Since the robot can only rotate left or right, turning around requires two consecutive rotations: first to the right, then another to the right, resulting in a full 180-degree turn. This is a standard interpretation of 'turn around' in a robot navigation context. The state shows no objects are discussed or being held, so no object-related actions are needed. The plan consists of two Motion(RotateRight) actions to achieve the desired orientation.
